animeOverseerCodes.title: Anime Overseer Codes
animeOverseerCodes.intro: Anime Overseer codes are the fastest starter boost for summoning units, upgrading your team, and saving reroll resources for stronger characters. New drops are usually tied to updates, likes, fixes, launch milestones, and official community announcements.

codes.bestFor: Early summons, upgrades, and trait preparation
Mixed utility rewards and starter upgrades

animeOverseerCodes.expired: No expired Anime Overseer codes are currently listed.
animeOverseerCodes.howToTitle: How to redeem Anime Overseer codes
howToSteps: Open Anime Overseer on Roblox and enter the game.
Look for the code redemption menu or settings icon inside the game.
Type or paste an active Anime Overseer code exactly as written.
Confirm the redemption to claim Gems, Gold, Trait Discs, and other rewards.

animeOverseerBeginnerGuide.title: Anime Overseer Beginner Guide
animeOverseerBeginnerGuide.intro: Anime Overseer is an Early Access Roblox anime strategy and tower-defense style game. The main goal is to gather a team, fight waves of enemies, improve your units, and evolve them into stronger versions for harder modes.

steps.title: Enter Early Access
steps.description: Anime Overseer is currently in Early Access. Players can access it through paid entry or wait in the AFK Chamber to gain access.
steps.tip: Start by checking the official Roblox page before joining, because Early Access rules can affect new players.

steps.title: Gather Your First Team
steps.description: Build a starter lineup before worrying about perfect units. A usable team matters more than one rare character at the beginning.
steps.tip: Use free Gems from codes to support early summons.

steps.title: Fight Enemy Waves
steps.description: The core gameplay is wave defense: place or use your team to stop enemies and push through harder stages.
steps.tip: Watch which wave ends your run, then upgrade around that weak point.

steps.title: Upgrade Units During Progression
steps.description: Gold, Coins, and other rewards help strengthen your account and improve unit performance over time.
steps.tip: Spend early currency on units that carry more than one wave instead of spreading upgrades too thin.

steps.title: Save Rare Reroll Resources
steps.description: Trait Rerolls, Trait Discs, Soul Crystals, and Super Soul Crystals are valuable rewards from codes.
steps.tip: Do not burn rare rerolls on units you expect to replace quickly.

steps.title: Evolve Strong Units
steps.description: The official description highlights unit evolution as a key way to unlock stronger powers.
steps.tip: Only invest evolution resources into units you plan to keep using.

steps.title: Play With Friends
steps.description: Anime Overseer supports playing together with friends in endless adventurous game modes.
steps.tip: Co-op play is useful when learning enemy waves and testing team setups.

quickTips: Redeem every active code early for a strong first-session boost.
A balanced team clears more waves than one overloaded carry.
Save Trait Rerolls and Soul Crystals for units you will keep.
Check the official Discord after milestones and updates for new code drops.

animeOverseerUnitsTierList.title: Anime Overseer Units Tier List
animeOverseerUnitsTierList.intro: Anime Overseer is built around gathering, upgrading, and evolving units to survive enemy waves. For early progression, the best units are the ones that clear waves efficiently, scale well with upgrades, and stay useful after evolution investment.

tiers.label: Top Carry Units
basis: Useful in harder or endless modes
tiers.useWhen: Build around these units first and protect rare reroll resources for them.

tiers.label: Reliable Core Units
tiers.role: Main team support or secondary DPS
basis: Useful across early and mid progression
tiers.useWhen: Use these to fill your main team while waiting for stronger pulls.

tiers.label: Early Progress Units
tiers.useWhen: Use these for early clears, but avoid spending premium rerolls too early.

tiers.label: Replaceable Units
basis: Not ideal for rare trait investment
tiers.useWhen: Use only until you pull better wave-clear or carry options.

tiers.label: Trait and Resource Targets
basis: Makes good use of Trait Discs
tiers.useWhen: Test performance first, then spend rerolls only if the unit stays in your team.

animeOverseerTraitsGuide.title: Anime Overseer Traits Guide
animeOverseerTraitsGuide.intro: Traits are one of the most important upgrade decisions in Anime Overseer because several active codes give Trait Discs, Disc Rerolls, and Trait Rerolls. The safest approach is to save premium reroll resources for units that already prove useful in waves, upgrades, and evolution planning.

cards.name: Trait Discs
cards.description: Used for trait-related progression and reroll preparation.
cards.bestUse: Save for units you plan to keep using beyond the first starter stages.

cards.name: Trait Rerolls
cards.description: Used to change or improve a unit trait.
cards.bestUse: Spend on high-value units after testing their damage, placement value, and upgrade scaling.

cards.name: Disc Rerolls
cards.description: A reroll reward type tied to trait improvement.
cards.bestUse: Keep for stronger units instead of using them on temporary filler units.

cards.name: Soul Crystals
cards.description: A progression material rewarded by codes and connected to stronger unit investment.
cards.bestUse: Pair with units that are worth long-term upgrade or evolution investment.

cards.name: Super Soul Crystals
cards.description: A higher-value soul material rewarded by several active codes.
cards.bestUse: Reserve for stronger team pieces instead of low-tier placeholders.

cards.name: Best Reroll Timing
cards.description: Prevents wasting rare reroll resources early.
cards.bestUse: First build a stable team, then reroll traits on your best carry or long-term evolved unit.

cards.name: Avoid Early Waste
cards.description: Keeps rare materials available for better units later.
cards.bestUse: Do not chase perfect traits before you know which units will stay in your main lineup.

animeOverseerEvolutionGuide.title: Anime Overseer Evolution Guide
animeOverseerEvolutionGuide.intro: Anime Overseer is built around gathering a team, fighting waves of enemies, upgrading units, and evolving them for stronger powers. Evolution should be treated as a major account investment, not something to rush on every early unit.

steps.title: Build a usable team first
steps.description: Start by filling your lineup with units that can reliably clear early enemy waves. A balanced team matters more than evolving one random unit too early.

steps.title: Upgrade before evolving
steps.description: Use Gold and battle rewards to improve units you actively use. Evolution has the most value when the base unit is already part of your main lineup.

steps.title: Save rare materials
steps.description: Do not spend Soul Crystals, Super Soul Crystals, Souls, Relic Shards, or special food items casually. These rewards appear in code drops and are better saved for units you plan to keep.

steps.title: Evolve priority units
steps.description: Choose units that help with harder waves, longer battles, or co-op runs. Evolution is strongest when it improves a unit that already carries your team.

steps.title: Test the evolved unit in waves
steps.description: After evolving, place the unit in real battles and compare clear speed, survival, and upgrade value. Keep investing only if the evolved unit improves your stage progress.

checklist: A stable main lineup is already clearing waves
Enough Gold, Soul Crystals, and food materials are saved
The unit has a clear long-term role on your team
The evolution improves harder wave or endless performance

animeOverseerGemsAndGoldFarming.title: Anime Overseer Gems and Gold Farming Guide
animeOverseerGemsAndGoldFarming.intro: Gems and Gold are the main early resources players look for because they support summons, upgrades, and faster roster growth. Anime Overseer code rewards also include Trait Discs, Soul Crystals, Super Soul Crystals, food items, and Relic Shards, so resource planning matters from the first session.

resources.tip: Use Gems when you are missing strong team options. Avoid spending all Gems before checking current banners or new update rewards.

resources.bestUse: Upgrading units and supporting early progression
resources.tip: Spend Gold on units you actually use in waves. Keep a reserve so you can upgrade better units after new pulls.
Use Trait Discs on long-term units, not temporary early-game fillers.

resources.bestUse: Advanced unit progression and evolution planning
resources.tip: Hold Soul Crystals until you know which evolved units fit your main team.
Treat Super Soul Crystals as rare materials and avoid spending them before choosing a core unit.
Keep Souls for units that stay useful in harder waves.
Items such as Meat, Onigiri, and Potato appear in reward drops, so keep them until a unit upgrade path needs them.
Save Relic Shards for a build that improves your wave clear or long-run performance.

animeOverseerGameModesAndWaves.title: Anime Overseer Game Modes and Waves Guide
animeOverseerGameModesAndWaves.intro: Anime Overseer focuses on fighting waves of enemies with a team of upgraded anime units. The official description highlights team building, waves, co-op play, endless adventurous game modes, unit upgrades, and evolution as the core gameplay loop.

faqs.question: What is the main battle loop in Anime Overseer?
faqs.answer: Gather units, enter battles, place or use your team against waves of enemies, upgrade your units, and push toward harder stages.
faqs.bestFor: New players learning what to do first

faqs.question: How should players prepare for harder waves?
faqs.answer: Bring units you have already upgraded, keep Gold ready for battle improvements, and avoid relying on only one strong unit. Harder waves reward a stronger full lineup.

faqs.question: Why does co-op matter in Anime Overseer?
faqs.answer: Anime Overseer supports playing with friends, which helps players handle longer fights and more demanding wave pressure with multiple lineups working together.

faqs.question: What should players do in endless-style modes?
faqs.answer: Use your strongest upgraded units, save resources for units that scale well over time, and test whether evolved units improve your long-run clear power.

faqs.question: When should players change their lineup?
faqs.answer: Change your team when waves take too long to clear, enemies leak through too often, or your upgrades no longer create a clear power boost.

animeOverseerUpdatesAndDiscord.title: Anime Overseer Updates and Discord Guide
animeOverseerUpdatesAndDiscord.intro: Anime Overseer is listed on Roblox as an Early Access game with paid access or AFK Chamber access, and the official description points players toward likes, updates, codes, team building, waves, multiplayer, upgrades, and evolution. The Discord server is the main community hub for updates, events, giveaways, guides, codes, and player help.

entries.label: Early Access
entries.title: Game access status
entries.description: Anime Overseer is marked as Early Access on Roblox. The game page states that players can enter through paid access or by waiting in the AFK Chamber.
entries.action: Check the Roblox game page before joining.

entries.label: Full Release
entries.title: Release signal
entries.description: The Roblox description says full release is coming soon, making update tracking important for new players and returning players.
entries.action: Watch the official Roblox page and Discord for release announcements.

entries.label: Codes
entries.title: Code announcement path
entries.description: The game page asks players to like the game for future updates and codes. The Discord server also presents itself as a place for codes and recent updates.
entries.action: Check Discord announcements and the Roblox community page after milestones or fixes.

entries.label: Community
entries.title: Discord hub
entries.description: The Anime Overseer Discord server is listed for active chat, events, giveaways, guides, codes, leaks, and player help.
entries.action: Use Discord for help, team advice, and update alerts.

entries.label: Gameplay Updates
entries.title: What to recheck after patches
entries.description: After updates, players should recheck unit upgrades, evolution materials, code rewards, and wave difficulty because these systems control progression.
entries.action: Refresh team plans after every major update.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Anime Overseer

questions.question: What is Anime Overseer?
questions.answer: Anime Overseer is a Roblox anime tower defense RPG in Early Access. You gather a team of anime units, fight enemy waves, upgrade units, evolve stronger forms, and use codes for faster early progression.

questions.question: How do I get free Anime Overseer codes?
questions.answer: Active Anime Overseer codes drop Gems, Gold, Trait Discs, Soul Crystals, Super Soul Crystals, food items, and Trait Rerolls. New codes are tied to updates, likes, fixes, and milestones, and are shared on the official Discord and Roblox community page.

questions.question: How do I access Anime Overseer in Early Access?
questions.answer: Anime Overseer is in Early Access on Roblox. You can enter through paid access or wait in the AFK Chamber to gain access. Always check the official Roblox game page for the current entry rules.

questions.question: Does Anime Overseer support co-op and multiplayer?
questions.answer: Yes. Anime Overseer supports playing with friends, which helps with longer fights, harder waves, and endless adventurous game modes where multiple lineups work together.

questions.question: How do I evolve units in Anime Overseer?
questions.answer: To evolve units in Anime Overseer, first build a stable team, upgrade the units you actively use, then spend rare materials like Soul Crystals and Super Soul Crystals only on units you plan to keep for harder waves and endless modes.

questions.question: Where can I play Anime Overseer?
questions.answer: Anime Overseer is available on Roblox. You can join through the official Roblox game page and follow the official Discord for updates, codes, events, and community guides.

cta.title: Ready to Play Anime Overseer?
cta.description: Join the Anime Overseer community on Discord for codes, updates, tier lists, and team-building tips!
